bibdata.title: The International System of Units
Recommended values of standard frequencies for applications including the practical realization of the metre and secondary representations of the second

Iodine (
λ
≈ 532 ⁢
nm
ii(lambda) ~~ 532 "unitsml(nm)")
Part 2.9
Appendix 2
Le système international d’unités
Valeurs recommandées des fréquences étalons destinées à la mise en pratique de la définition du mètre et aux représentations secondaires de la seconde

Iodine (
λ
≈ 532 ⁢
nm
ii(lambda) ~~ 532 "unitsml(nm)")
Partie 2.9
Appendice 2
bibdata.docidentifier: BIPM SI MEP M REC 532nm-2.9 A2

organization.name: Bureau International des Poids et Mesures

clause.title: Copyright statement
clause.p: This document is distributed under the terms of the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (), which permits unrestricted use, distribution, and reproduction in any medium, provided you give appropriate credit to the original author(s) and the source, provide a link to the Creative Commons license, and indicate if changes were made.

feedback-statement.p:
BIPM
Pavillon de Breteuil
F-92312 Sèvres Cedex
FRANCE

p.strong:
Absorbing molecule 127I2,
a 10
"a"_{10} component, R(56) 32-0 transition

fn.p:
All transitions in I2 refer to the
B 3
Π
0 u +
−
X 1
Σ g +
"B"^3Pi" "0_u^+ - X^1 Sigma_g^+ system.

clause.title: CIPM recommended values
clause.p:
with a relative standard uncertainty of
8.9 ×
10 12
8.9 xx 10^(12) apply to the radiation of a frequency-doubled Nd:YAG laser, stabilized with an iodine cell external to the laser, subject to the conditions:

li.p:
cold-finger temperature
( – 15 ± 1 )
⁢
°C
(–15 +- 1) "unitsml(degC)"
For the specification of operating conditions, such as temperature, modulation width and laser power, the symbols
±
pm refer to a tolerance, not an uncertainty.

frequency modulation width, peak-to-peak,
( 1 ± 0.2 )
⁢
MHz
(1 +- 0.2) "unitsml(MHz)" for
3 f
3f detection cases;

saturating beam intensity of
( 17 ± 11 )
⁢
mW
⁢
cm
− 2
(17 +- 11) "unitsml(mW*cm^(-2))"

clause.title: Source data

tr.th: source data

clause.p:
The standard uncertainty calculated from the dispersion of the three values is
2.7 ⁢
kHz
2.7 "unitsml(kHz)". Taking into account the frequency dependence on the cell quality and other effects, the CCL preferred to adopt a standard uncertainty of
5 ⁢
kHz
5 "unitsml(kHz)", corresponding to a relative standard uncertainty of
8.9 ×
10
− 12
8.9 xx 10^(-12).

Since 2001, it was noted that the global mean has changed from …​..513.2 to …​..511.5
kHz
"unitsml(kHz)", with a standard deviation of
2.6 ⁢
kHz
2.6 "unitsml(kHz)". Given the possible shifts due to beam alignment, etalon effects and other technical effects, it was decided not to change the 2001 value or uncertainty but rather to define more comprehensively the operating conditions as follows:

li.p:
cold-finger temperature
( – 15 ± 1 )
⁢
°C
(–15 +- 1) "unitsml(degC)"
For the specification of operating conditions, such as temperature, modulation width and laser power, the symbols
±
pm refer to a tolerance, not an uncertainty.

frequency modulation width, peak-to-peak,
( 1 ± 0.2 )
⁢
MHz
(1 +- 0.2) "unitsml(MHz)" for
3 f
3f detection cases;

saturating beam intensity of
( 17 ± 11 )
⁢
mW
⁢
cm
− 2
(17 +- 11) "unitsml(mW*cm^(-2))"

clause.p:
Other 127I2 absorbing transitions close to this transition may also be used by making reference to the following frequency differences, using the
a 10
"a"_{10} component of the R(56) 32-0 transition as a reference, see also source data :

where
f
( y , x )
f(y,x) represents the frequency of the transition denoted
y , x
y,x and
f
(
a 10
, R(56) 32-0 )
f("a"_{10}, " R(56)" "32-0") the frequency of the reference transition. The CCL preferred to assign an uncertainty of
5 ⁢
kHz
5 "unitsml(kHz)" to all listed frequency differences, regarding the possible influence of the quality of the iodine cell, background slopes and the small number of data for each frequency difference available.
In 2007 the CIPM at its 96th meeting on a proposition of the CCL recommended (Recommendation 1; CI-2007) that the above list shall be extended to the following lines

tr.th: Ref.

clause.p:
at a cold-finger temperature of
– 5 ⁢
°C
–5 "unitsml(degC)" (iodine pressure =
2.46 ⁢
Pa
2.46 "unitsml(Pa)")
For the iodine cold-finger temperature to iodine pressure conversion the formula derived by Gillespie and Fraser has been used. .

at a cold-finger temperature of
– 5 ⁢
°C
–5 "unitsml(degC)" (iodine pressure =
2.46 ⁢
Pa
2.46 "unitsml(Pa)").
These two measurements have been carried out with the same iodine cell. Therefore, the CCL decided to consider the arithmetic mean of these two data, i.e.

For a reference temperature of
– 15 ⁢
°C
–15 "unitsml(degC)" (iodine pressure =
0.83 ⁢
Pa
0.83 "unitsml(Pa)"), using a pressure dependence of
– 4.2 ⁢
kHz
⁢
Pa
− 1
–4.2 "unitsml(kHz/Pa)" , a correction of
+ 6.8 ⁢
kHz
+6.8 "unitsml(kHz)" has to be applied, giving

The following values have been obtained for the frequency differences between several 127I2 absorbing transitions and the R(56) 32-0 transition, at an iodine cold-finger temperature of
– 15 ⁢
°C
–15 "unitsml(degC)" (iodine pressure =
0.83 ⁢
Pa
0.83 "unitsml(Pa)"):

tr.th: unw. mean

clause.p:
where
f
( y , x )
f(y,x) represents the frequency of the transition denoted
y , x
y,x and
f
(
a 10
, R
( 56 )
32 − 0 )
f("a"_{10}, R(56) 32-0) the frequency of the reference transition.

clause.title: Absolute frequency of the other transitions related to those adopted as recommended and frequency intervals between transitions and hyperfine components
clause.p: These tables replace those published in BIPM Com. Cons. Long., 2001, 10, 151-167 and Metrologia, 2003, 40, 116-120.
The notation for the transitions and the components is that used in the source references. The values adopted for the frequency intervals are the weighted means of the values given in the references.
For the uncertainties, account has been taken of:

li.p: the uncertainties given by the authors;
the spread in the different determinations of a single component;
the effect of any perturbing components;
the difference between the calculated and the measured values.

clause.p:
In the tables,
u c
u_c represents the estimated combined standard uncertainty (
1
σ
1 ii(sigma)).
All transitions in molecular iodine refer to the B-X system.

bibitem.formattedref:
Diddams S. A., Jones D. J., Ye J., Cundiff S. T., Hall J. L., Ranka J. K., Windeler R. S., Holzwarth R., Udem T., Hänsch T. W., Direct Link between Microwave and Optical Frequencies with a
300 ⁢
THz
300 "unitsml(THz)" Femtosecond Laser Comb, Phys. Rev. Lett., 2000, 84, 5102-5105.
Ye J., Ma Long Sheng, Hall J. L., Molecular Iodine Clock, Phys. Rev. Lett., 2001, 87, 270801/1-4.

Sugiyama K., Onae A., Hong F.-L., Inaba H., Slyusarev S. N., Ikegami T., Ishikawa J., Minoshima K., Matsumoto H., Knight J. C., Wadsworth W. J., Russel P. St. J., Optical frequency measurement using an ultrafast mode-locked laser at NMIJ/AIST, 6th Symposium on Frequency Standards and Metrology, Ed. Gill P, World Scientific (Singapore), 2002, 427-434.

Holzwarth R., Nevsky A. Yu., Zimmermann M., Udem Th., Hänsch T. W., von Zanthier J., Walther H., Knight J. C., Wadsworth W. J., Russel P. St. R., Skvortsov M. N., Bagayev S. N., Absolute frequency measurement of iodine lines with a femtosecond optical synthesizer, Appl. Phys. B, 2001, 73, 269-271.

Nevsky A. Yu., Holzwarth R., Reichert J., Udem Th., Hänsch T. W., von Zanthier J., Walther H., Schnatz H., Riehle F., Pokasov P. V., Skvortsov M. N., Bagayev S. N., Frequency comparison and absolute frequency measurement of I2-stabilized lasers at
532 ⁢
nm
532 "unitsml(nm)", Optics Commun., 2001, 192, 263-272.
Gillespie L. J., Fraser L. A. D., J. Am. Chem. Soc., 1936, 58, 2260-2263.

Ye J., Robertsson L., Picard S., Ma L.-S., Hall J. L., Absolute Frequency Atlas of Molecular I2 Lines at
532 ⁢
nm
532 "unitsml(nm)", IEEE. Trans. Intrum. Meas., 1999, 48, 544-549.

Zhang Y., Ishikawa J., Hong F.-L., Accurate frequency atlas of molecular iodine near
532 ⁢
nm
532 "unitsml(nm)" measured by an optical frequency comb generator, Opt. Commun., 2001, 200, 209-215.
Recommendation CCL3 (BIPM Com. Cons. Long., 10th Meeting, 2001) adopted by the Comité International des Poids et Mesures at its 91th Meeting as Recommendation 1 (CI-2002).

Hong F.-L., Zhang Y., Ishikawa J., Onae A., Matsumoto H., Vibration dependence of the tensor spin-spin and scalar spin-spin hyperfine interactions by precision measurement of hyperfine structures of 127I2 near
532 ⁢
nm
532 "unitsml(nm)", J. Opt. Soc. Am. B., 2001, 19, 946-953.

Hong F.-L., Ishikawa J., Onae A., Matsumoto H., Rotation dependence of the excited-state electric quadrupole hyperfine interaction by high-resolution laser spectroscopy of 127I2, J. Opt. Soc. Am. B., 2001, 18, 1416-1422.

Hong F.-L., Ishikawa J., Hyperfine structures of the R(122) 35-0 and P(84) 33-0 transitions of 127I2 near
532 ⁢
nm
532 "unitsml(nm)", Opt. Commun., 2000, 183, 101-108.
Macfarlane G. M., Barwood G. P., Rowley W. R. C., Gill P., Interferometric Frequency Measurements of an Iodine Stabilized Nd:YAG laser, IEEE. Trans. Intrum. Meas., 1999, 48, 600-603.
Arie A., Byer R. L., The hyperfine structure of the 127I2 P(119) 35-0 transition, Opt. Commun., 1994, 111, 253-258 and Arie A., Byer R. L., Erratum, Opt. Commun., 1996, 127, 382.
Eickhoff M. L., Thesis, University of Colorado, 1994.

Arie A., Byer R. L., Laser heterodyne spectroscopy of 127I2 hyperfine structure near
532 ⁢
nm
532 "unitsml(nm)", J. Opt. Soc. Am., B, 1993, 10, 1990-1997, and A. Arie, R. L. Byer, Errata, J. Opt. Soc. Am. B, 1994, 11, 866.

Eickhoff M. L. and Hall J. L., Optical Frequency Standard at
532 ⁢
nm
532 "unitsml(nm)", IEEE Trans. Instrum. Meas., 1995, 44, 155-158.

Jungner P., Eickhoff M. L., Swartz S. D., Ye Jun, Hall J. L., Waltman S., Stability and absolute frequency of molecular iodine transitions near
532 ⁢
nm
532 "unitsml(nm)", Laser Frequency Stabilization and Noise Reduction, SPIE, 1995, 2378, 22-34.

Jungner P. A., Swartz S. D., Eickhoff M., Ye J., Hall J. L., Waltman S., Absolute Frequency of the Molecular Iodine Transitions R(56)32-0 Near
532 ⁢
nm
532 "unitsml(nm)", IEEE trans. Instrum. Meas., 1995, 44, 151-154.
Robertsson L., Ma L.-S., Picard S., Improved Iodine-Stabilized Nd:YAG Lasers, Laser Frequency Stabilization, Standards, Measurement, and Applications, Proceedings of SPIE, 2000, 4269, 268-271.

Picard S., Robertsson L., Ma L.-S., Nyholm K., Merimaa M., Ahola T. E., Balling P., Křen P., Wallerand J.-P., International comparison of 127I2-stabilized frequency-doubled Nd:YAG lasers between the BIPM, the MIKES, the BNM-INM and the CMI, May 2001, Appl. Opt., 2003, 42, 1019-1028 and CCL/MePWG/2001-07.BIPM.

Hong F.-L., Ye J., Ma L.-S., Picard S., Bordé Ch. J., Hall J. L., Rotation dependence of electric quadrupole hyperfine interaction in the ground state of molecular iodine by high-resolution laser spectroscopy, J. Opt. Soc. Am. B, 2001, 18, 379-387.
Quinn T. J., Practical realization of the definition of the metre (1997), Metrologia, 36, 1999, 211-244.

Procès-Verbaux des Séances du Comité International des Poids et Mesures, 96th meeting (2007) 2008, Recommendation 1 (CI-2007): Revision of the Mise en pratique list of recommended radiations. p. 185 (see ).
Report of the 13th meeting (13 – 14 September 2007) of the Consultative Committee for Length (CCL) to the International Committee for Weights and Measures p. 34 -35 (see e.g. ).

Hong F.-L., Zhang Y., Ishikawa J., Onae A., Matsumoto H., Hyperfine structure and absolute frequency determination of the R(121)35-0 and P(142)37-0 transitions of 127I2 near
532 ⁢
nm
532 "unitsml(nm)", Opt. Commun. 2002, 212, 89–95.

Hong F.-L., Diddams S., Guo R., Bi Z.-Y., Onae A., Inaba H., Ishikawa J., Okumura K., Katsuragi D., Hirata J., Shimizu T., Kurosu T., Koga Y., Matsumoto H., Frequency measurements and hyperfine structure of the R(85)33– 0 transition of molecular iodine with a femtosecond optical comb, J. Opt. Soc. Am. B, 2004, 21, 88-95.
